element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary combat interaction; replaces jumping-on-enemies paradigm from SMB1
Multi-room level structure becomes primary navigation method
Coin and mushroom collection; optional world warps unlocked
Four characters with distinct jump height, speed, strength, and float ability create strategic choices

element_introduction_order.element: Health system (hearts instead of one-hit death)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multiple damage tolerance; health replenishment via defeated enemy drops
Environmental hazard; timing and vertical navigation critical
Momentum and control challenges on frictionless terrain
Screen-wide enemy clear utility; one-use resource

typical_relief_triggers: Collecting heart drops from defeated enemies
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple strong enemies in single room
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multi-room structure allows micro-pacing: tension builds within rooms during enemy encounters and hazard navigation; relief occurs between rooms via doors. No time limit permits player-controlled rhythm, but boss gates and world structure create natural crescendos.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Jar entries leading to alternate rooms; Sub-space potion locations hidden in ground; some rooms only accessible via specific character abilities (Luigi's high jump, Princess's float)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Magic potions in seemingly dead-end rooms open Sub-space passages; certain jars in Sub-space warp to later worlds; mushroom blocks and hidden items accessible only to specific characters

difficulty_curve.early_game: World 1-2: Introduction of core mechanics (pulling, throwing, climbing). Character selection accommodates varied skill levels. Birdo and Mouser bosses teach basic pattern recognition without overwhelming difficulty.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: World 3-5: Environmental hazards increase (quicksand, ice, varied vertical/horizontal scrolling). Boss patterns become more complex. Sub-space warps unlock alternative routing. Character ability optimization becomes strategically important.
difficulty_curve.late_game: World 6-7: Multiple strong enemy types per room. Complex multi-stage boss encounters. Vertical navigation hazards. Final confrontation with Wart requires precise pattern execution. No warp path exists to final boss.
notable_spikes: Birdo introduction (World 1): First boss requires learning projectile-avoidance and egg-throwing mechanics
Mouser encounter (World 2): Introduces faster pattern, POW Block dependency
Quicksand sections: Precision timing required; falling resets progress
Wart final boss: Complex multi-phase pattern; cannot be damaged by vegetables (requires potion/bomb strategy)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level divided into 3-5 connected rooms accessed via doors. Encourages exploration and optional path discovery. Allows micro-tension cycles within single level.
Certain rooms or shortcuts accessible only to specific characters (e.g., Luigi high-jump required, Princess float required). Incentivizes character selection strategy and replay.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World 1-1 (noted in TASVideos: high platform reachable by Luigi or via Toad double-jump shortcut)

notable_level_patterns.description: Potion-activated Sub-space contains reflected level geometry. Serves as safe collection zone and warp gate. Visual contrast signals departure from primary threat space.
Each world theme (desert, snow, sky, etc.) introduces signature hazard type (quicksand, ice, wind). Forces adaptation of core mechanics to new constraints.
Single-room encounter at world end. Pattern-based attack sequence. Requires mastery of vegetable-throwing and positioning. Distinct pacing break from exploration.
